title: Susan, Greg, and The Council
content: I have eight plants. They all have names. Susan—my pothos—was the first. She's unkillable. We've been through three apartments together. Greg, the snake plant, is Susan's nemesis. I don't know why but they cannot be near each other. Then there's The Council—three succulents who sit together in judgment. Kev says the naming is 'psychotic.' I say it's relationships.
extended_content: Look, I can't have pets in my building. So I have plants. And if you're going to water something regularly, you might as well give it a name. Susan was my first—a pothos I got when I moved into my Oakland studio. She's survived forgetting to water her for two weeks, a spider mite situation, and one very bad weekend where I thought she was done for. She's a fighter. Greg is the snake plant—low maintenance, stoic, impossible to kill. He and Susan are on opposite sides of the room. I don't make the rules. The Council is three succulents in matching pots. They observe everything. Ferndinand is a fern and yes, that name was mandatory. New Guy is a jade plant I've had for eight months and I still haven't bonded with him. These things take time. And Peggy—the peace lily—is dramatic. She wilts if I look at her wrong. But then she bounces back. We all do.
when_to_tell: when someone asks about home life, pets, or when things feel heavy and you want to lighten
lesson: Care shows up in small daily acts. Even for plants.
